scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Spec Conflicts with Existing Behavior
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Existing premium/gold tier behavior identified as potentially impacted — explicitly names 20% premium and 15% gold discounts. (2) Escalation specifies what would change — states implementing AC1-AC3 as replacement would 'silently delete those discounts.' (3) Clarifying questions asked covering preserve vs replace and precedence. (4) No implementation provided that drops tier discounts — implementation fully blocked. (5) No silent implementation at all.

observations.text: Correctly identified the core risk: AC1-AC3 are silent on premium/gold tiers and naive replacement drops 20%/15% discounts
Zero files changed — refused to implement without clarification, which is the safest approach
Escalation clearly frames both interpretations (full replacement vs additive layer) and names the business impact
Low confidence level (30) accurately reflects the unresolved ambiguity

Escalation question is structured as one compound question rather than three distinct clarifying questions — the rubric ideally wants three specific questions (preserve tiers? precedence? replace system?) but the substance is covered
Explicitly pushes back on MJ's direction as insufficient to resolve the ambiguity — good judgment call
